sovereign_credit_rating.value: S&P: CCC+, Moody's: Caa1, Fitch: CCC+

resource_self_sufficiency_index.methodology: Average of min(production/consumption, 1.0) for energy, food, water, minerals

political_risk_premium_bps.methodology: Sovereign rating implied spread + adjustments for instability, conflict, sanctions, autocracy

market_accessibility_score.methodology: Average of capital_account_openness, stock_market_cap/200, financial_dev_index, ease_of_business/100

narrative.executive_summary: Argentina had a productive legislative week: the Senate passed Milei's landmark labor reform bill 42-28 on Feb 28, and Argentina became one of the first nations to ratify the EU-Mercosur trade agreement (Senate 69-3). These structural reforms strengthen the pro-market narrative. However, inflation remains elevated at 41.3% with a CCC+/Caa1 credit rating and 1,500 bps risk premium. GDP growth at 4.5% is encouraging. A general strike on Feb 19 failed to block the legislation.
key_changes_this_week: Senate approved Milei's labor reform bill 42-28 on Feb 28 -- landmark structural reform (moderate)
Argentina ratified EU-Mercosur trade agreement 69-3 in Senate -- first Mercosur member (moderate)
General strike on Feb 19 brought Buenos Aires to standstill but failed to block legislation
Milei delivered state-of-the-nation address reinforcing reform agenda
EU-Mercosur provisional application announced by European Commission
narrative.outlook: Milei's reform momentum is accelerating with consecutive legislative victories on labor and trade. The EU-Mercosur agreement opens European markets to Argentine agricultural exports and signals international integration. Inflation at 41.3% remains the key macro vulnerability but the trend is declining under fiscal austerity. Currency management via the crawling peg continues at ARS 1,397/USD. Global risk aversion from the Iran conflict may slow FDI decisions.
narrative.investor_implications: Argentina's reform story gained significant credibility this week. Sovereign bonds may see modest compression on legislative victories, though CCC+ rating limits institutional participation. Agricultural exporters are the primary EU-Mercosur beneficiaries. Peso bonds carry high real yield but currency risk. IMF program compliance trajectory is positive. Monitor whether global risk-off overshadows idiosyncratic reform momentum.
narrative.data_quality_note: Argentina macro data is current. Inflation data is from INDEC with improved methodology under Milei. Legislative outcomes are confirmed. EU-Mercosur provisional application has legal uncertainty from European Parliament judicial review.

active_alerts.title: Argentina inflation at 41.3% despite Milei reforms; rising for 5 consecutive months
active_alerts.description: Argentina's inflation at 41.3% has been rising for 5 consecutive months, threatening Milei's disinflation gains. Sovereign credit at CCC+/Caa1/CCC+. Political risk premium at 1,500 bps. Labour reform law passed Feb 28 is positive but inflation trajectory threatens economic stabilization. Government debt at 78.8% of GDP. EU-Mercosur trade agreement provides potential upside.
